questions.question: What is the purpose of the redirection operator « > », when used with the standard file « stdout »?
options: B) Appends to the original file
questions.explanation: When used with stdout, the redirection operator > overwrites the original file.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the redirection operator « << », when used with the standard file « stdin »?
options: B) Appends to the original file
questions.explanation: When used with stdin, the redirection operator << appends to the original file.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the redirection operator « >> », when used with the standard file « stdout »?
options: B) Appends to the original file
questions.explanation: When used with stdout, the redirection operator >> appends to the original file.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the redirection operator « < », when used with the standard file « stdin »?
options: B) Appends to the original file
questions.explanation: When used with stdin, the redirection operator < creates a new file.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the redirection operator « 2>> », when used with the standard file « stderr »?
options: B) Appends to the original file
questions.explanation: When used with stderr, the redirection operator 2>> appends to the original file.
